texto: The next time it begins to rain ... lie down on your belly nestle your chin into the grass and get a frog's-eye view of how raindrops fall . . . The sight of hundreds of blades of grass bowing down and popping back up like piano keys strikes me as one of the merriest sights in the world.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Malcolm Margolin: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “The next time it begins to rain ... lie down on your belly nestle your chin into the grass and ge…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, nature, grass pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dia-chuvoso e reflexao.

frase_original: The next time it begins to rain ... lie down on your belly nestle your chin into the grass and get a frog's-eye view of how raindrops fall . . . The sight of hundreds of blades of grass bowing down and popping back up like piano keys strikes me as one of the merriest sights in the world.
